objective: C3A-CP8 implements the next Checkpoint 3A child checkpoint: an executable real-browser E2E probe for the local/mock controlled-demo path with no success-path API or route interception. Positive claims: C3A-CP8-HARNESS-001 dispatches the real-browser E2E probe as an implemented executable Playwright probe with subprocess.run shell=False and bounded/redacted failure output while preserving CP1 API E2E, CP2 output-correctness, CP3 language-quality, CP4 media-artifacts, CP5 access/quota/retention, CP6 security/observability, and CP7 performance probes; C3A-CP8-BROWSER-001 launches the local frontend against the local backend, creates approved synthetic project knowledge through the user-visible workflow, uploads/approves/ingests it, generates grounded walkthrough output, and renders public-safe run/request/source/eval/artifact/observability evidence in the browser; C3A-CP8-NO-INTERCEPTION-001 proves docs-only/prose/static-snapshot checks, API-only tests, route/network interception that fabricates success, canned success files, success-shaped browser state without runtime nonce/source/run binding, missing run/request/source/eval binding, stale evidence, and cross-project replay cannot satisfy the browser probe; C3A-CP8-REDACTION-001 keeps raw uploaded content, prompt-injection text, private-looking markers, sensitive tokens, provider payloads, generated full script text, and browser/server timeout failures bounded and redacted. This remains Checkpoint 3A only and does not authorize Checkpoint 3B, Checkpoint 3C, hosted/public demo, provider setup, cloned identity, real media, or production readiness. Repository-ledger cleanup for PR #268 and issue #267 may be bundled because this is a substantive child checkpoint rather than a standalone status-only follow-up.
